texto: Once, I took the penny whistle you gave me and discovered a spotby the roaring falls where I could play as loud as I wanted. I lay in the bifurcated trunkof a low-slung birch tree. The sun peeked through applauding leaves, high overhead.

texto: what if there was an uncanny moment when all the birds were grounded from Cape Town to Juneau, and everywhere between--all feathers frozen in a universal stutter, so quick as to make a snail of light, and even Stephen Hawking's mind would miss it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kristen Henderson que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “what if there was an uncanny moment when all the birds were grounded from Cape Town to Juneau, an…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, birds, cape-town, com foco em luto e cape-town.
